summary.htmlContent:
Quality Council Meeting Summary

Top 3 Problems Identified

  • High number of overdue effectiveness cheques and product reviews.
  • Ongoing issues with TI (Temporary Instructions) and lack of a clear plan to reach 0 count.
  • Resource constraints and risks in M building, including audit readiness and storage practices.

Key actions

  • Formalising operator process and process knowledge sessions.
  • Follow up on overdue long-term actions, especially for engineering and deviation owners.
  • Planning periodic reviews 3 months in advance to address delays in parallel factory.
  • Track QIP actions for OSD for 2026 in quality council.
  • Catch up between Nuwandi and Chris about ongoing DI deviations.
  • Raise change control for M building to manage audit risks.
  • Upskill operators and run process knowledge sessions.

Decisions Made

  • Keep high number of effectiveness cheques overdue in place as a hot topic.
  • Remove certain actions not relevant for compliance (e.g., general training piece).
  • Flagging risk around production equipment reports and critical alarms.
  • Proceed with triage and approval for M building in third week of January.

Escalations Required

  • Escalate TI issues through Psychology Council for alternative solutions.
  • Escalate long-term solution required for temporary instructions from Maurizio or central quality.
  • Escalate resource risks for commissioning of M building if not resolved by January.

Suggestions for Process Confirmations for Next Month

  • Continue formalising operator process and process knowledge sessions.
  • Ensure periodic reviews are planned well in advance.
  • Track and follow up on overdue actions and DI deviations.
  • Review and optimize curriculum and training modules, especially for operators and PTLS.
  • Monitor and address audit risks in M building.

Communications to cascade to team

  • Quality KPI review and cascade through Barry as SLT member.
  • Communicate changes in classification of actions and long-term action tracking.
  • Remind team of ongoing process knowledge sessions and upskilling opportunities.
  • Update on management monitoring completion and remaining actions.
  • Notify about Laura’s sabbatical and potential work stream impacts.
